methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

transformations: Assets converted to shares via _convertToShares using exchange rate
Exchange rate updated after mint via _updateExchangeRate
data_flows.trust_changes: User trusts vault with assets; vault trusts yield vault with deposits
exposure_risks: Exchange rate can be observed by frontrunners
manipulation_risks: Pre-existing vault balance allows free deposit - if assets sit in vault, next depositor uses them without paying, stealing available yield

transformations: Prize tokens contributed to PrizePool accumulator
Vault shares minted to liquidation account
Yield fee tracked in _yieldFeeTotalSupply
data_flows.trust_changes: Vault trusts liquidation pair contract; PrizePool trusts vault for contribution accounting
exposure_risks: Yield fee calculation can revert with division by zero when yieldFeePercentage == FEE_PRECISION
manipulation_risks: Vault asset balance comparison with shares (_amountOut >= _vaultAssets) is comparing incompatible units, potentially leaving assets in vault for theft

transformations: Raw contribution amount → exponentially smoothed disbursement via alpha parameter
Balance delta check against prizeToken.balanceOf
data_flows.trust_changes: Anyone can call contributePrizeTokens for any vault, but must have transferred tokens first
manipulation_risks: Front-running closeDraw with contributions to manipulate vault portion for prize claims

transformations: Contributions for draw → deltaPrizeTokensPerShare (floor division)
Tier liquidity reclamation from shrunk tiers
Reserve updated with reclaimed + remainder
exposure_risks: uint96 truncation of _contributionsForDraw silently loses contributions exceeding ~79 billion tokens
manipulation_risks: Tier expansion can bypass thresholds if canary tier is claimed (largestTierClaimed + 2 >= numTiers regardless of claim counts)

transformations: PRN = keccak256(user, tier, prizeIndex, winningRandomNumber)
TWAB lookup for user balance and total supply
Vault portion from accumulator
Winning zone = userTwab * tierOdds * vaultPortion
constrainedRandom = PRN % vaultTwabTotalSupply
data_flows.trust_changes: Vault trusts claimer (msg.sender check); PrizePool trusts vault (msg.sender as vault); hooks can redirect prize recipient
exposure_risks: Hook implementations can grief claimers by reverting
manipulation_risks: beforeClaimPrize hook can redirect prize to arbitrary address
Stale vault portion if contributions change between draw close and claim

transformations: Balance changes recorded as observations in ring buffer
Cumulative balance extrapolated from previous observation
Period-based observation indexing
data_flows.trust_changes: msg.sender is used as vault address - any contract can act as a vault
manipulation_risks: No access control on mint/burn/transfer - any contract can create its own 'vault' namespace, but this is by design

transformations: Fee computed via VRGDA: price = minFee * e^(decayConstant * (elapsed - sold/perTimeUnit))
Fee averaged across all claims in batch
Fee truncated to uint96
data_flows.trust_changes: Claimer trusts prizePool state (claimCount, numberOfTiers) at call time
exposure_risks: Fee computation depends on elapsed time and claimed count, both publicly observable
manipulation_risks: Race condition: multiple claimers competing, claimCount changes between fee computation and claim execution, potentially making fee calculation stale

core_rules: Prize pool token balance >= tier liquidity + reserve + claimer rewards
Each winner can claim each prize index only once per draw
Number of tiers must be between MINIMUM_NUMBER_OF_TIERS (3) and MAXIMUM_NUMBER_OF_TIERS (15)
Canary tier is always the last tier (numberOfTiers - 1)
Vault exchange rate caps at last recorded rate to exclude yield from share pricing
TWAB observations stored in ring buffer with MAX_CARDINALITY = 365
economic_incentives: Claimers incentivized by VRGDA fees that increase over time, creating urgency to claim
Yield fee recipient earns a percentage of liquidated yield
Vaults compete for prize allocation proportional to their contributions
Canary tier serves as an indicator for tier expansion/contraction
governance_mechanisms: DrawManager role controls draw closure and reserve withdrawal
Vault owner controls claimer, liquidation pair, yield fee settings
setDrawManager is one-time settable but front-runnable

edge_cases.scenario: Vault yield fee percentage set to exactly FEE_PRECISION (1e9, i.e., 100%)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should either be disallowed or handle gracefully
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Division by zero in liquidate(): (_amountOut * FEE_PRECISION) / (FEE_PRECISION - _yieldFeePercentage) where denominator is 0
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Owner sets yieldFeePercentage to FEE_PRECISION, permanently breaking liquidation and prize funding. Even if not malicious, a configuration mistake causes permanent DoS.

edge_cases.scenario: Assets sitting in Vault's balance (from direct transfer or liquidation leftover) when a deposit occurs
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Depositor should pay for all shares received; vault balance should not subsidize deposits
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In _deposit(), if _assets <= vault's asset balance, no transfer from caller occurs. Caller receives shares backed by vault's pre-existing balance, effectively stealing available yield.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker sends underlying asset to vault, then calls deposit() to receive shares without paying. The stolen amount equals the vault's asset balance. In liquidation flows where _amountOut < _vaultAssets (comparing shares to assets - unit mismatch), assets remain in vault enabling this attack.

edge_cases.scenario: Canary tier prize claimed, bypassing expansion threshold checks
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Tier expansion should require both canary claim count AND normal claim count to meet claimExpansionThreshold
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In _computeNextNumberOfTiers, _nextNumberOfTiers = largestTierClaimed + 2. If canary (tier numberOfTiers-1) is claimed, _nextNumberOfTiers = numberOfTiers + 1 BEFORE the threshold check. Even if thresholds are not met, _nextNumberOfTiers remains numberOfTiers + 1 since the threshold check only sets (doesn't reduce) _nextNumberOfTiers.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A single canary claim can trigger tier expansion regardless of whether the threshold conditions are met, leading to premature and unwarranted tier expansion that dilutes prize liquidity across too many tiers.

edge_cases.scenario: Contributions exceed uint96 max in closeDraw
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All contributed tokens should be distributed across tiers
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint96(_contributionsForDraw(lastClosedDrawId + 1)) silently truncates values > type(uint96).max (~79.2 billion with 18 decimals), losing the excess contributions
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: In high-value prize pools with large contributions, excess tokens beyond uint96 capacity are lost from distribution, remaining unaccounted in the prize pool balance

edge_cases.scenario: Vault liquidate() compares _amountOut (shares) >= _vaultAssets (asset amount) for yield vault deposit trigger
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should compare equivalent units (both in asset terms or both in share terms)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: When exchange rate != 1:1, the comparison is between different units. If exchange rate > 1 (more assets per share), _amountOut < _vaultAssets even when the economic value is equal, causing assets to remain in vault.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Assets accumulate in vault over time when exchange rate drifts from 1:1, enabling the free deposit vulnerability described above

edge_cases.scenario: Prize claims for early draws attempt TWAB lookups before protocol start
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Graceful handling of insufficient TWAB history
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _getVaultUserBalanceAndTotalSupplyTwab computes _startTimestamp = _endTimestamp - _drawDuration * drawPeriodSeconds. For high-frequency tiers (grand prize: 365 draws), this underflows uint32 when insufficient draws have occurred, causing revert.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not exploitable but prevents legitimate grand prize claims for ~365 draws after launch. By design for TWAB accuracy but undocumented.

edge_cases.scenario: Winner sets beforeClaimPrize hook that always reverts
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only that winner's claim fails; others succeed
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Vault.claimPrizes batches all claims. If one hook reverts, the entire batch reverts, preventing all winners in the batch from being claimed.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Griefing attack: winner sets reverting hook, forcing claimers to exclude them from batches. Claimers waste gas on failed transactions before discovering the issue.

edge_cases.scenario: PrizePool.setDrawManager called when drawManager is address(0)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Anyone can call setDrawManager to set themselves as draw manager via front-running, gaining control of draw closure and reserve withdrawal
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Front-run the constructor's DrawManagerSet to become the draw manager and control draw closures and reserve withdrawals. Mitigated by post-deployment verification.

edge_cases.scenario: UD34x4 precision loss accumulates in _consumeLiquidity over many prize claims
edge_cases.actual_behavior: fromUD60x18toUD34x4 divides by 1e14, losing 14 decimal places of precision. Each consumption operation loses up to 1e14 wei, accumulating over hundreds of claims per draw.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Over many claims, tracked consumption is less than actual consumption, creating a small phantom liquidity that can never be claimed. Reserve may be over-consumed to compensate.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Liquidity distributed proportionally with minimal loss
edge_cases.actual_behavior: floor() truncates fractional tokens per share. With large totalShares, the remainder (up to totalShares - 1 tokens) is added to reserve, slightly reducing tier allocations each draw.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Not directly exploitable but causes systematic under-allocation to prize tiers in favor of the reserve over many draws.

edge_cases.scenario: Vault liquidation approves underlying asset to LiquidationPair
edge_cases.expected_behavior: LP should only need prize token approval or no approval at all
edge_cases.actual_behavior: setLiquidationPair approves type(uint256).max of the underlying asset to the new LP address. A malicious LP can drain the vault's underlying asset balance.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If owner is compromised or sets a malicious LP, the LP can call transferFrom to steal all underlying assets held by the vault. Trusted owner assumption mitigates but doesn't eliminate the risk.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Vault portion should only reflect contributions up to the closed draw
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _computeVaultTierDetails passes _lastClosedDrawId + 1 as endDrawId to _getVaultPortion, including smoothed disbursements from the open draw in the vault portion calculation
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A vault can contribute to the open draw immediately before claiming, slightly inflating its portion for the closed draw's prize calculation. Economic feasibility is limited by contribution cost.

assumption_violations: Vault assumes no assets will be directly transferred to it, but anyone can do so via ERC20 transfer, enabling the free deposit exploit
TieredLiquidityDistributor assumes _nextNumberOfTiers will be capped by threshold checks, but the initial value from largestTierClaimed + 2 can bypass this
Vault assumes yieldFeePercentage < FEE_PRECISION for liquidation math, but _setYieldFeePercentage allows equality
PrizePool assumes contributions fit in uint96, but the accumulator operates in uint256
Vault._deposit assumes vault asset balance is available for depositor use, conflating yield-related balances with deposit flows
game_theory_risks: Claimers compete in a first-come basis; earlier claimers get lower VRGDA fees, incentivizing gas auction behavior
A vault can contribute large amounts to inflate its portion just before prize claims, then withdraw contributions next draw
Winners with hooks can grief claimers by setting reverting hooks, then claiming directly themselves (if claim function allows self-claiming)
Tier expansion can be manipulated by ensuring canary prizes are claimed (even a single claim triggers expansion), diluting prizes across more tiers
integration_risks: Yield vault (ERC4626) maxWithdraw may not reflect actual withdrawable amount if yield vault has withdrawal fees or delays
TwabController period alignment assumes consistent block.timestamp progression; timestamp manipulation could affect TWAB calculations
DrawAccumulatorLib exponential smoothing with extreme alpha values (close to 1) can cause numerical instability in pow() calculations
External hook contracts (IVaultHooks) have unbounded gas consumption, potentially causing out-of-gas for claimers
calculation_errors: Vault.liquidate yield fee: (_amountOut * FEE_PRECISION) / (FEE_PRECISION - _yieldFeePercentage) division by zero when fee = 100%
Vault.liquidate asset sweep: comparison _amountOut >= _vaultAssets mixes share units with asset units
_computeNextNumberOfTiers: _nextNumberOfTiers = largestTierClaimed + 2 can exceed _numTiers without meeting threshold conditions
_getVaultUserBalanceAndTotalSupplyTwab: uint32 timestamp underflow when _drawDuration * drawPeriodSeconds exceeds _endTimestamp

token_semantics.notes: Used as prize and contribution token. SafeERC20 used for transfers. contributePrizeTokens relies on balanceOf delta - fee-on-transfer tokens would break accounting.
ERC4626 + ERC20Permit. Balances stored in TwabController, not standard ERC20 balanceOf. Minted during deposits and liquidations. uint96 cap from TwabController.

Decimals determined at deployment via ERC4626. Fee-on-transfer tokens would break _deposit logic (transfers less than expected). Approved to both yield vault (max) and liquidation pair (max, unnecessarily).

invariants.name: accumulator_available_disbursed_sum
invariants.notes: For any new observation: available + disbursed should equal sum of all contributions accounting for smoothing
